Install from https://litestream.io/\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n    log_info \"✓ litestream: $(litestream version 2>&1 | head -1)\"\n\n    # Check sqlite3\n    if ! command -v sqlite3 &> /dev/null; then\n        log_error \"sqlite3 not found. Install sqlite3 (version 3.40+)\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n    log_info \"✓ sqlite3: $(sqlite3 --version | cut -d' ' -f1)\"\n\n    # Check ARMOR connectivity (optional - may be configured later)\n    if [[ -n \"${ARMOR_ENDPOINT:-}\" ]]; then\n        if ! curl -s \"http://${ARMOR_ENDPOINT}/healthz\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n            log_warn \"ARMOR endpoint ${ARMOR_ENDPOINT} not reachable from host\"\n            log_warn \"Connectivity will be tested from within the restore job\"\n        else\n            log_info \"✓ ARMOR endpoint reachable\"\n        fi\n    fi\n}\n\n# Validate arguments\nvalidate_args() {\n    if [[ $# -ne 3 ]]; then\n        log_error \"Usage: $0 <bucket> <database-path> <output-path>\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"Arguments:\"\n        echo \"  bucket          - ARMOR bucket name (e.g., kalshi-tape, kalshi-weather)\"\n        echo \"  database-path   - Path to database in bucket (e.g., /data/sensors.db)\"\n        echo \"  output-path     - Local path for restored database (e.g., /tmp/sensors.db)\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"Example:\"\n        echo \"  $0 kalshi-tape /data/sensors.db /tmp/restored-sensors.db\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n\n    BUCKET=\"$1\"\n    DB_PATH=\"$2\"\n    OUTPUT_PATH=\"$3\"\n\n    # Create output directory if needed\n    OUTPUT_DIR=$(dirname \"$OUTPUT_PATH\")\n    if [[ ! -d \"$OUTPUT_DIR\" ]]; then\n        log_info \"Creating output directory: $OUTPUT_DIR\"\n        mkdir -p \"$OUTPUT_DIR\"\n    fi\n\n    log_info \"Restore configuration:\"\n    log_info \"  Bucket: $BUCKET\"\n    log_info \"  Database path: $DB_PATH\"\n    log_info \"  Output path: $OUTPUT_PATH\"\n}\n\n# Load ARMOR credentials from config file or environment\nload_credentials() {\n    # Check if credentials are already set in environment\n    if [[ -n \"${ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY:-}\" && -n \"${ARMOR_SECRET_KEY:-}\" ]]; then\n        log_info \"Using credentials from environment\"\n    elif [[ -f \"$HOME/.config/armor/credentials\" ]]; then\n        log_info \"Loading credentials from ~/.config/armor/credentials\"\n        # Source credentials file (expected format: ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY=xxx ARMOR_SECRET_KEY=yyy)\n        source \"$HOME/.config/armor/credentials\"\n    else\n        log_error \"ARMOR credentials not found. Set ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY and ARMOR_SECRET_KEY environment variables\"\n        log_error \"Or create ~/.config/armor/credentials file\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n\n    # Check master encryption key\n    if [[ -z \"${ARMOR_MEK:-}\" ]]; then\n        if [[ -f \"$HOME/.config/armor/mek\" ]]; then\n            log_info \"Loading MEK from ~/.config/armor/mek\"\n            ARMOR_MEK=$(cat \"$HOME/.config/armor/mek\")\n        else\n            log_error \"Master encryption key (MEK) not found\"\n            log_error \"Set ARMOR_MEK environment variable or create ~/.config/armor/mek\"\n            exit 1\n        fi\n    fi\n\n    export ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY ARMOR_SECRET_KEY ARMOR_MEK\n\n    # Set defaults\n    export ARMOR_ENDPOINT=\"${ARMOR_ENDPOINT:-armor.armor.svc:9000}\"\n    export ARMOR_REGION=\"${ARMOR_REGION:-us-east-005}\"\n\n    log_info \"ARMOR configuration:\"\n    log_info \"  Endpoint: $ARMOR_ENDPOINT\"\n    log_info \"  Region: $ARMOR_REGION\"\n    log_info \"  Access key: ${ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY:0:8}...\"\n}\n\n# Build litestream restore URL\nbuild_restore_url() {\n    # ARMOR S3-compatible endpoint URL\n    # litestream uses s3:// protocol with custom endpoint\n    RESTORE_URL=\"s3://${BUCKET}${DB_PATH}?endpoint=http://${ARMOR_ENDPOINT}\"\n\n    log_info \"Restore URL: s3://${BUCKET}${DB_PATH}\"\n}\n\n# Execute restore\nexecute_restore() {\n    log_info \"Starting database restore...\"\n\n    # Set environment variables for litestream\n    export LITESTREAM_ACCESS_KEY_ID=\"$ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY\"\n    export LITESTREAM_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=\"$ARMOR_SECRET_KEY\"\n\n    # Run litestream restore\n    # This decrypts and reconstructs the database from ARMOR blocks\n    if litestream restore -o \"$OUTPUT_PATH\" \"$RESTORE_URL\"; then\n        echo \"Status: SUCCESS\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"You can now open the database with:\"\n        echo \"  sqlite3 $OUTPUT_PATH\"\n    else\n        echo \"Status: FAILED\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"Check the error messages above and verify:\"\n        echo \"  1. ARMOR credentials are correct\"\n        echo \"  2. Bucket and database path exist\"\n        echo \"  3. Network connectivity to ARMOR endpoint\"\n        echo \"  4. Master encryption key (MEK) matches\"\n    fi\n    echo \"==========================================\"\n}\n\n# Main execution\nmain() {\n    log_info \"ARMOR Database Restore Script\"\n    echo \"\"\n\n    check_requirements\n    echo \"\"\n\n    validate_args \"$@\"\n    echo \"\"\n\n    load_credentials\n    echo \"\"\n\n    build_restore_url\n    echo \"\"\n\n    execute_restore\n    local EXIT_CODE=$?\n\n    print_summary $EXIT_CODE\n    exit $EXIT_CODE\n}\n\n# Run main function\nmain \"$@\"\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"440dcfbe-d7f7-479e-8bb4-4d44c768200b","uuid":"d0d245f3-0a59-482e-978d-104fa5a5c732"}

Install from https://litestream.io/\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n    log_info \"✓ litestream: $(litestream version 2>&1 | head -1)\"\n\n    # Check sqlite3\n    if ! command -v sqlite3 &> /dev/null; then\n        log_error \"sqlite3 not found. Install sqlite3 (version 3.40+)\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n    log_info \"✓ sqlite3: $(sqlite3 --version | cut -d' ' -f1)\"\n\n    # Check ARMOR connectivity (optional - may be configured later)\n    if [[ -n \"${ARMOR_ENDPOINT:-}\" ]]; then\n        if ! curl -s \"http://${ARMOR_ENDPOINT}/healthz\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n            log_warn \"ARMOR endpoint ${ARMOR_ENDPOINT} not reachable from host\"\n            log_warn \"Connectivity will be tested from within the restore job\"\n        else\n            log_info \"✓ ARMOR endpoint reachable\"\n        fi\n    fi\n}\n\n# Validate arguments\nvalidate_args() {\n    if [[ $# -ne 3 ]]; then\n        log_error \"Usage: $0 <bucket> <database-path> <output-path>\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"Arguments:\"\n        echo \"  bucket          - ARMOR bucket name (e.g., kalshi-tape, kalshi-weather)\"\n        echo \"  database-path   - Path to database in bucket (e.g., /data/sensors.db)\"\n        echo \"  output-path     - Local path for restored database (e.g., /tmp/sensors.db)\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"Example:\"\n        echo \"  $0 kalshi-tape /data/sensors.db /tmp/restored-sensors.db\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n\n    BUCKET=\"$1\"\n    DB_PATH=\"$2\"\n    OUTPUT_PATH=\"$3\"\n\n    # Create output directory if needed\n    OUTPUT_DIR=$(dirname \"$OUTPUT_PATH\")\n    if [[ ! then\n        if [[ -f \"$HOME/.config/armor/mek\" ]]; then\n            log_info \"Loading MEK from ~/.config/armor/mek\"\n            ARMOR_MEK=$(cat \"$HOME/.config/armor/mek\")\n        else\n            log_error \"Master encryption key (MEK) not found\"\n            log_error \"Set ARMOR_MEK environment variable or create ~/.config/armor/mek\"\n            exit 1\n        fi\n    fi\n\n    export ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY ARMOR_SECRET_KEY ARMOR_MEK\n\n    # Set defaults\n    export ARMOR_ENDPOINT=\"${ARMOR_ENDPOINT:-armor.armor.svc:9000}\"\n    export ARMOR_REGION=\"${ARMOR_REGION:-us-east-005}\"\n\n    log_info \"ARMOR configuration:\"\n    log_info \"  Endpoint: $ARMOR_ENDPOINT\"\n    log_info \"  Region: $ARMOR_REGION\"\n    log_info \"  Access key: ${ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY:0:8}...\"\n}\n\n# Build litestream restore URL\nbuild_restore_url() {\n    # ARMOR S3-compatible endpoint URL\n    # litestream uses s3:// protocol with custom endpoint\n    RESTORE_URL=\"s3://${BUCKET}${DB_PATH}?endpoint=http://${ARMOR_ENDPOINT}\"\n\n    log_info \"Restore URL: s3://${BUCKET}${DB_PATH}\"\n}\n\n# Execute restore\nexecute_restore() {\n    log_info \"Starting database restore...\"\n\n    # Set environment variables for litestream\n    export LITESTREAM_ACCESS_KEY_ID=\"$ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY\"\n    export LITESTREAM_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=\"$ARMOR_SECRET_KEY\"\n\n    # Run litestream restore\n    # This decrypts and reconstructs the database from ARMOR blocks\n    if litestream restore -o \"$OUTPUT_PATH\" \"$RESTORE_URL\"; then\n        echo \"Status: SUCCESS\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"You can now open the database with:\"\n        echo \"  sqlite3 $OUTPUT_PATH\"\n    else\n        echo \"Status: FAILED\"\n        echo \"\"\n        echo \"Check the error messages above and verify:\"\n        echo \"  1. ARMOR credentials are correct\"\n        echo \"  2. Bucket and database path exist\"\n        echo \"  3. Network connectivity to ARMOR endpoint\"\n        echo \"  4. Master encryption key (MEK) matches\"\n    fi\n    echo \"==========================================\"\n}\n\n# Main execution\nmain() {\n    log_info \"ARMOR Database Restore Script\"\n    echo \"\"\n\n    check_requirements\n    echo \"\"\n\n    validate_args \"$@\"\n    echo \"\"\n\n    load_credentials\n    echo \"\"\n\n    build_restore_url\n    echo \"\"\n\n    execute_restore\n    local EXIT_CODE=$?\n\n    print_summary $EXIT_CODE\n    exit $EXIT_CODE\n}\n\n# Run main function\nmain \"$@\"\nEOF\nchmod +x /home/coding/scratch/fresh-restore/restore.sh\n","description":"Create restore.sh script with executable permissions"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"440dcfbe-d7f7-479e-8bb4-4d44c768200b","uuid":"e0e3686c-83b1-4cf2-b38b-f56dc43cb1f3"}

This restore infrastructure allows you to recover databases from ARMOR buckets.\n\n## Prerequisites Verification\n\n### 1. Restore Script\n\nThe main restore script is `restore.sh`:\n```bash\n./restore.sh <bucket> <database-path> <output-path>\n```\n\n**Status:** ✓ Created and executable\n```bash\nls -la restore.sh\n# -rwxr-xr-x 1 coding users 7764 Jul 15 12:00 restore.sh\n```\n\n### 2. litestream Binary\n\n**Status:** ✓ Installed (development build)\n```bash\nwhich litestream\n# /home/coding/.local/bin/litestream\n\nlitestream version\n# (development build)\n```\n\n### 3. sqlite3\n\n**Status:** ✓ Installed (version 3.48.0)\n```bash\nwhich sqlite3\n# /home/coding/.nix-profile/bin/sqlite3\n\nsqlite3 --version\n# 3.48.0 2025-01-14 11:05:00 ...\n```\n\n### 4. ARMOR Endpoint Connectivity\n\n**ARMOR Deployment:**\n- **Cluster:** rs-manager (Rackspace Spot, us-east-iad-1)\n- **Namespace:** armor\n- **Service:** `armor.armor.svc:9000` (S3 API), `:9001` (Admin API)\n- **Pod:** `armor-596fdf4f47-w642j` (Running, 17 days uptime)\n\n**Connectivity Verification:**\n\nTo verify ARMOR connectivity from within the cluster:\n\n```bash\n# Option 1: From a pod in the armor namespace\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-rs-manager:8001 run -n armor --rm curl-test --image=curlimages/curl --restart=Never -- curl -s http://armor:9000/healthz\n\n# Option 2: Port-forward (requires read-write kubeconfig)\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/path/to/rs-manager.kubeconfig port-forward -n armor svc/armor 9000:9000 9001:9001\n\n# Option 3: Check pod status\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-rs-manager:8001 get pods -n armor\n```\n\n**Note:** The read-only proxy (`traefik-rs-manager:8001`) does not allow port-forwarding. For full connectivity testing, use:\n1. A temporary pod within the cluster (Option 1)\n2. Direct kubeconfig with cluster-admin access (Option 2)\n\n## ARMOR Service Endpoints\n\n| Service | Port | Purpose |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| S3 API | 9000 | Main ARMOR endpoint for S3-compatible operations |\n| Admin API | 9001 | Dashboard and administrative interface |\n| Health Check | `/healthz` on 9000 | Liveness probe endpoint |\n| Readiness Check | `/readyz` on 9000 | Readiness probe endpoint |\n\n## Credential Setup\n\nCreate the ARMOR credential files (outside of git):\n\n```bash\n# Directory structure\nmkdir -p ~/.config/armor\n\n# Credentials file (ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY and ARMOR_SECRET_KEY)\ncat > ~/.config/armor/credentials <<EOF\nARMOR_ACCESS_KEY=\"your-access-key\"\nARMOR_SECRET_KEY=\"your-secret-key\"\nEOF\n\n# Master encryption key\ncat > ~/.config/armor/mek <<EOF\nyour-master-encryption-key-hex-string\nEOF\n```\n\n## Restore Example\n\n```bash\n# Restore kalshi-tape sensors.db\n./restore.sh kalshi-tape /data/sensors.db /tmp/restored-sensors.db\n\n# Restore kalshi-weather weather.db\n./restore.sh kalshi-weather /data/weather.db /tmp/restored-weather.db\n```\n\n## Environment Variables\n\nThe restore script accepts these environment variables:\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n| ARMOR_ENDPOINT | No | `armor.armor.svc:9000` | ARMOR server endpoint |\n| ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY | Yes* | - | S3 access key ID (* or from credentials file) |\n| ARMOR_SECRET_KEY | Yes* | - | S3 secret access key (* or from credentials file) |\n| ARMOR_REGION | No | `us-east-005` | B2 region |\n| ARMOR_MEK | Yes* | - | Master encryption key (* or from ~/.config/armor/mek) |\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **litestream restore** connects to ARMOR using S3-compatible protocol\n2. ARMOR serves encrypted blocks from Backblaze B2 storage\n3. litestream decrypts blocks using the MEK (Master Encryption Key)\n4. Database is reconstructed from WAL segments and checkpoint data\n5. **sqlite3 integrity_check** verifies the restored database\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### \"ARMOR endpoint not reachable from host\"\n\nThis is expected when running the script from your local machine. ARMOR endpoints are cluster-internal. Use:\n- A pod within the cluster for connectivity testing\n- Set up a Kubernetes job that runs the restore within the cluster\n\n### \"Master encryption key (MEK) not found\"\n\nEnsure `~/.config/armor/mek` exists or set `ARMOR_MEK` environment variable.\n\n### \"Database integrity check failed\"\n\nThe restore may be incomplete. Verify:\n1. Bucket and database path exist in ARMOR\n2. MEK matches the one used for encryption\n3. Network connectivity to ARMOR is stable\n\n## Related ARMOR Infrastructure\n\n- **ARMOR Repository:** https://github.com/jedarden/ARMOR\n- **Restore Verifier:** Runs periodic integrity checks on stored databases\n- **Buckets:** armor-apexalgo, ord-devimprint, iad-ci, iad-kalshi, rs-manager\n\n## Next Steps\n\n1. Set up ARMOR credentials in `~/.config/armor/`\n2. Test connectivity using a temporary pod\n3. Run a test restore using the restore.sh script\n4. This restore infrastructure allows you to recover databases from ARMOR buckets.\n\n## Prerequisites Verification\n\n### 1. Restore Script\n\nThe main restore script is `restore.sh`:\n```bash\n./restore.sh <bucket> <database-path> <output-path>\n```\n\n**Status:** ✓ Created and executable\n```bash\nls -la restore.sh\n# -rwxr-xr-x 1 coding users 7764 Jul 15 12:00 restore.sh\n```\n\n### 2. litestream Binary\n\n**Status:** ✓ Installed (development build)\n```bash\nwhich litestream\n# /home/coding/.local/bin/litestream\n\nlitestream version\n# (development build)\n```\n\n### 3. sqlite3\n\n**Status:** ✓ Installed (version 3.48.0)\n```bash\nwhich sqlite3\n# /home/coding/.nix-profile/bin/sqlite3\n\nsqlite3 --version\n# 3.48.0 2025-01-14 11:05:00 ...\n```\n\n### 4. ARMOR Endpoint Connectivity\n\n**ARMOR Deployment:**\n- **Cluster:** rs-manager (Rackspace Spot, us-east-iad-1)\n- **Namespace:** armor\n- **Service:** `armor.armor.svc:9000` (S3 API), `:9001` (Admin API)\n- **Pod:** `armor-596fdf4f47-w642j` (Running, 17 days uptime)\n\n**Connectivity Verification:**\n\nTo verify ARMOR connectivity from within the cluster:\n\n```bash\n# Option 1: From a pod in the armor namespace\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-rs-manager:8001 run -n armor --rm curl-test --image=curlimages/curl --restart=Never -- curl -s http://armor:9000/healthz\n\n# Option 2: Port-forward (requires read-write kubeconfig)\nkubectl --kubeconfig=/path/to/rs-manager.kubeconfig port-forward -n armor svc/armor 9000:9000 9001:9001\n\n# Option 3: Check pod status\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-rs-manager:8001 get pods -n armor\n```\n\n**Note:** The read-only proxy (`traefik-rs-manager:8001`) does not allow port-forwarding. For full connectivity testing, use:\n1. A temporary pod within the cluster (Option 1)\n2. Direct kubeconfig with cluster-admin access (Option 2)\n\n## ARMOR Service Endpoints\n\n| Service | Port | Purpose |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| S3 API | 9000 | Main ARMOR endpoint for S3-compatible operations |\n| Admin API | 9001 | Dashboard and administrative interface |\n| Health Check | `/healthz` on 9000 | Liveness probe endpoint |\n| Readiness Check | `/readyz` on 9000 | Readiness probe endpoint |\n\n## Credential Setup\n\nCreate the ARMOR credential files (outside of git):\n\n```bash\n# Directory structure\nmkdir -p ~/.config/armor\n\n# Credentials file (ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY and ARMOR_SECRET_KEY)\ncat > ~/.config/armor/credentials <<EOF\nARMOR_ACCESS_KEY=\"your-access-key\"\nARMOR_SECRET_KEY=\"your-secret-key\"\nEOF\n\n# Master encryption key\ncat > ~/.config/armor/mek <<EOF\nyour-master-encryption-key-hex-string\nEOF\n```\n\n## Restore Example\n\n```bash\n# Restore kalshi-tape sensors.db\n./restore.sh kalshi-tape /data/sensors.db /tmp/restored-sensors.db\n\n# Restore kalshi-weather weather.db\n./restore.sh kalshi-weather /data/weather.db /tmp/restored-weather.db\n```\n\n## Environment Variables\n\nThe restore script accepts these environment variables:\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n| ARMOR_ENDPOINT | No | `armor.armor.svc:9000` | ARMOR server endpoint |\n| ARMOR_ACCESS_KEY | Yes* | - | S3 access key ID (* or from credentials file) |\n| ARMOR_SECRET_KEY | Yes* | - | S3 secret access key (* or from credentials file) |\n| ARMOR_REGION | No | `us-east-005` | B2 region |\n| ARMOR_MEK | Yes* | - | Master encryption key (* or from ~/.config/armor/mek) |\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **litestream restore** connects to ARMOR using S3-compatible protocol\n2. ARMOR serves encrypted blocks from Backblaze B2 storage\n3. litestream decrypts blocks using the MEK (Master Encryption Key)\n4. Database is reconstructed from WAL segments and checkpoint data\n5. **sqlite3 integrity_check** verifies the restored database\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### \"ARMOR endpoint not reachable from host\"\n\nThis is expected when running the script from your local machine. ARMOR endpoints are cluster-internal. Use:\n- A pod within the cluster for connectivity testing\n- Set up a Kubernetes job that runs the restore within the cluster\n\n### \"Master encryption key (MEK) not found\"\n\nEnsure `~/.config/armor/mek` exists or set `ARMOR_MEK` environment variable.\n\n### \"Database integrity check failed\"\n\nThe restore may be incomplete. Verify:\n1. Bucket and database path exist in ARMOR\n2. MEK matches the one used for encryption\n3. Network connectivity to ARMOR is stable\n\n## Related ARMOR Infrastructure\n\n- **ARMOR Repository:** https://github.com/jedarden/ARMOR\n- **Restore Verifier:** Runs periodic integrity checks on stored databases\n- **Buckets:** armor-apexalgo, ord-devimprint, iad-ci, iad-kalshi, rs-manager\n\n## Next Steps\n\n1. Set up ARMOR credentials in `~/.config/armor/`\n2. Test connectivity using a temporary pod\n3. Run a test restore using the restore.sh script\n4.
